fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKFourByteTag` represents a packed four-byte (32-bit) identifier commonly used in OpenType
font tables and Skia API parameters. Each byte corresponds to one ASCII character. The value
is packed as `(c1 << 24) | (c2 << 16) | (c3 << 8) | c4`.

Use the character constructor `SKFourByteTag(char, char, char, char)` to build a tag from
individual characters. Use <xref:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag.Parse(System.String)> to parse a
tag from a string: strings shorter than four characters are right-padded with spaces, and
strings longer than four characters are truncated.

## Examples

```csharp
// Create the 'wght' weight-axis tag from individual characters.
var wght = new SKFourByteTag('w', 'g', 'h', 't');

// Parse from a string.
var wdth = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wdth");

// Implicit conversion to/from uint.
uint raw = wght;
SKFourByteTag same = raw;
Console.WriteLine(same == wght); // True
```
]]></format>
fields.summary: Represents a packed four-byte identifier used in OpenType font tables and Skia API parameters.
Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> struct from a packed 32-bit value.
Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> struct from four ASCII characters.

fields.returns: An <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> representing the first four characters of <paramref name="tag" />.
fields.summary: Creates an <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> by parsing the first four characters of a string.

params.tag: A string of up to four characters. Strings shorter than four characters are right-padded with spaces; strings longer than four characters are truncated.

fields.returns: A four-character string whose characters correspond to the four bytes of the tag value.
fields.summary: Returns the string representation of the tag as a four-character string.
